plan_history.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 (Serial0/1/0) is DOWN because an inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5/224.0.0.6). Goal: allow OSPF hellos so adjacency to R1 forms.
plan_history.problem: Inbound ACL R2_IN applied to interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 contains explicit deny entries for OSPF multicast addresses 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6, blocking OSPF Hellos and preventing adjacency formation with R1.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the two explicit deny entries for 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6 from ACL R2_IN on router2 so OSPF multicast traffic is permitted; risk: this will allow OSPF multicast through the interface and may permit other multicast traffic previously blocked.

plan_history.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 Serial0/1/0 is DOWN because inbound ACL R2_IN denies OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6); goal: allow OSPF hellos so adjacency to R1 forms.
plan_history.problem: ACL R2_IN on router2 contains explicit deny entries for multicast addresses 224.0.0.5 (seq 10) and 224.0.0.6 (seq 20) applied inbound on Serial0/1/0, dropping OSPF hellos and preventing adjacency.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the two deny entries (seq 10 and seq 20) from ACL R2_IN on router2 so OSPF multicast is permitted to Serial0/1/0. Operational risk: briefly permitting multicast traffic on the interface; if Serial0/1/0 is absent, adjacency will still fail and an interface restoration will be required.

remaining_issues: OSPF adjacency on R2 Serial0/1/0 is still not forming — get_ospf shows no neighbor on that interface.

Running-config for router2 does not list Serial0/1/0 under Serial interfaces (get_running_config lists only 0/1/1), suggesting the interface may be absent from the config; adjacency will remain down until the interface is restored/configured into OSPF.

There is an inconsistency between the running-config (no Serial0/1/0) and the interface operational status (up/up); reconcile whether the interface config was removed or an alternate state is present.
